display.purpose: The cinematic focal tier and only fluid one. Scoreboard scores clamp(3.5rem,13vw,5.5rem); page hero (masthead) clamp(3.25rem,10vw,5.75rem). Ceiling 5.75rem, never past 6rem.

headline.purpose: Section titles, round labels, feature player names. Barlow Condensed, fixed size.

body.purpose: Analysis prose and explanations. Inter 400, max 70ch.

label.purpose: Chips, status markers, data-column headers. Uppercase only for true headers/chips.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Interactive cards and bracket nodes on hover; paired with translateY(-2px). The only resting shadow is none.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus. Neutral ink ring on a black offset — never a host color, to protect the red = live rule.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for entrances, count-ups, and bracket reveals. Exponential ease-out, no bounce.

motion.name: dur-ui
motion.purpose: Working-UI state transitions (hover, focus, selection). Users are in flow.

motion.name: dur-cinematic
motion.purpose: Focal-moment choreography only — bracket draw, score count-up, champion reveal.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Neutral high-contrast action. Host colors are reserved for data, never buttons.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #080c0f; background: #f3f5f8; border: none; border-radius: 4px; padding: 10px 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #080c0f, 0 0 0 4px #f3f5f8; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Ghost action: transparent with a structural border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #f3f5f8; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #292e34; border-radius: 4px; padding: 10px 20px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #181e23; border-color: #3a4047; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #080c0f, 0 0 0 4px #f3f5f8; }

components.name: Live Chip
components.description: Live/now marker (e.g. a match kicking off Today). Red text on a red tint plus a leading pulsing dot — red's role-locked live use; the dot is the non-color signal.
components.css: .ds-chip-live { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #ed4a49; background: color-mix(in oklab, #ed4a49 12%, transparent); border-radius: 2px; padding: 5px 10px; } .ds-chip-live__dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #ed4a49; animation: ds-pulse 1.6s cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1) infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.3; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-chip-live__dot { animation: none; } }

components.name: Group Chip
components.description: Group-stage category marker — green's boldest on-screen presence. The letter is a filled green badge (the per-group differentiator); groups are not hue-coded individually.
components.css: .ds-chip-group { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #35c26d; } .ds-chip-group__badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 22px; height: 22px; font-weight: 800; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #080c0f; background: #35c26d; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Match Card
components.description: Core matchup container, stadium-scoreboard layout: FIFA code above the full team name, with an oversized tabular score that dominates the card. Flat at rest.
components.html:
ARGArgentina2
BRABrazil1
components.css: .ds-match-card { background: #101419; border: 1px solid #292e34; border-radius: 8px; padding: 32px; min-width: 280px; } .ds-match-card__row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr auto; align-items: center; gap: 16px; padding: 8px 0; } .ds-match-card__id { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-match-card__code { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.6875rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #8a9096; } .ds-match-card__team { font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', 'Arial Narrow', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 2rem; line-height: 0.95; text-transform: uppercase; color: #f3f5f8; } .ds-match-card__score { font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', 'Arial Narrow', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 4.5rem; line-height: 0.85; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #f3f5f8; } .ds-match-card__score.is-loser { color: #8a9096; }

components.name: Probability Bar
components.description: Home/Draw/Away outcome bar. Blue marks the prediction channel; neutrals carry the rest. Every segment is labeled, so it is never color-only.
components.html:
53%
27%
20%
components.css: .ds-prob { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 28px; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-prob__seg { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; color: #080c0f; min-width: 0; } .ds-prob__seg--home { background: #439bf7; } .ds-prob__seg--draw { background: #8a9096; } .ds-prob__seg--away { background: #a5acb2; }

narrative.overview: A deep black studio surface. Three host nation colors holding down three precise data roles — Mexican green for group-stage data, Canadian red for live indicators, American blue for predictions and probability. Trophy gold appearing exactly once per landmark moment. The interface feels like the analyst desk at ESPN or a FIFA broadcast production: high contrast, unhurried authority, every element earning its placement through function. The system is cinematic without being theatrical — motion serves the drama of the game, but the everyday UI loads instantly into the task, and the cinematic register is reserved for focal moments like a match score or a championship.
keyCharacteristics: 70%+ deep cool-black surface (#080c0f, OKLCH 15% 0.01 250) — everything else is signal
Three host nation colors, three locked data roles, zero decorative use
Barlow Condensed display + Inter body, all numerics in tabular-nums
Motion anchored to real events; the cinematic tier is saved for focal moments
Interactive chrome is neutral — color belongs to data, never to buttons

rules.name: The 70% Rule
rules.body: Studio Black covers at least 70% of any view. A new surface element belongs on the black ground, not beside a competing surface.

rules.name: The Host Nation Rule
rules.body: Three accent colors, three data roles, no exceptions: green = group data, red = live, blue = prediction. Color never appears outside its role; interactive chrome is neutral.

rules.name: The Color-Blind Contract
rules.body: No data channel uses color as its only differentiator. Each host color pairs with shape, label, position, or pattern, and the three are separated in lightness to aid CVD.

rules.name: The Rarity Gradient
rules.body: Trophy gold > host accents > neutral text. The more chromatic a color, the rarer its use. Any accent crossing ~20% of a screen is noise.

rules.name: The Broadcast Weight Rule
rules.body: Barlow Condensed is reserved for scores, team names, and match headlines. Anything read rather than scanned is Inter.

rules.name: The Tabular Precision Rule
rules.body: All numerics use font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums. A number that shifts column width as it updates is a credibility failure.

rules.name: The Fixed-Scale Rule
rules.body: Only the Display tier scales with clamp. Headline through Label are fixed rem; product UI should not shrink headings inside a panel.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest; depth is tonal. A shadow visible without interaction is decorative — remove it.

dos: Do keep Studio Black (#080c0f) at 70%+ of any view.
Do use Barlow Condensed only for scores, team names, and match headlines; Inter for everything read or scanned.
Do keep the host colors role-locked: green = group, red = live, blue = prediction. Neutral chrome for buttons and nav.
Do reserve trophy gold for one genuine championship moment per view (e.g. the masthead favourite).
Do concentrate blue in the probability bar; render secondary probability figures (championship odds) in neutral ink so blue stays rare.
Do render the group identity as a bold filled green badge — green's primary on-screen presence — differentiated by letter, not per-group hues.
Do pair every color-encoded datum with a non-color signal (dot, label, letter, position).
Do set font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums on all numerics.
Do keep motion anchored to events; 150-250ms on working UI, choreography only for focal moments.
Do test every data visualization in deuteranopia/protanopia simulation.
donts: Don't use the host accent colors decoratively — a red CTA or green hover is a violation.
Don't use teal, purple, or gradient combinations (the Vite template's #aa3bff purple is exactly what this system rejects).
Don't add glow, neon, or cyberpunk RGB; dark must read as broadcast studio, not gaming rig.
Don't build generic SaaS chrome: sidebar navs, icon-heading-text card grids, blue-accent BI layouts.
Don't use the sportsbook aesthetic — no dark-green-and-gold odds bars, no betting-slip chips.
Don't let visualizations look like Jupyter output — no matplotlib frames or academic histogram defaults.
Don't dump dense tables as the first answer; lead with the narrative, offer the table as drill-down.
Don't ship resting-state shadows, border-left stripe accents, or gradient text.
Don't rely on color alone; if a chart breaks when desaturated, it fails the Color-Blind Contract.
